idea.hook: Could a single teaspoon weigh more than a mountain?
facts: When a massive star collapses, it leaves behind a neutron star, typically only 20 km (12.4 miles) wide.
Despite its tiny size, a neutron star packs 1.4 to 2.1 times the mass of our Sun!
Its density is so extreme that just ONE TEASPOON (5 ml) of its material would weigh over 5 BILLION TONS (5 x 10^12 kg).
idea.payoff: That's roughly the weight of all the cars on Earth combined... TWICE! Making neutron stars one of the densest objects in the universe.
idea.title: A Teaspoon of Neutron Star?! 🤯✨

scenes.visual: A stylized graphic showing a small teaspoon on one side of a scale and a large mountain silhouette on the other, with the scale tipping heavily towards the teaspoon. Background is a dark cosmic scene.
A detailed outline of a large city (e.g., Manhattan Island) appears, with a tiny, glowing neutron star icon precisely scaled to 20 km placed within it to show its minuscule size.

scenes.text: Packs 1.4 to 2.1x the mass of our Sun!
scenes.visual: A large, bright Sun icon appears on the left. A much smaller, dense neutron star icon appears on the right. An animated arrow points from the neutron star to the Sun, displaying 'x1.4 - x2.1' to emphasize mass comparison.

A single, metallic teaspoon graphic appears and fills with a shimmering, super-dense material. Below it, a giant stack of abstract weight blocks (or a large stylized number '5,000,000,000 TONS') materializes, symbolizing immense weight.

scenes.text: That's 2x All Cars on Earth! Densest in the Universe.
scenes.visual: A multitude of simplified car icons animate, filling the screen, then quickly compress and combine. A large 'x2' symbol appears, followed by a final, highly detailed and slowly rotating neutron star, glowing intensely in the center, representing its extreme density.
